text: The last port is placed either before or after creation of the pericardiotomy.
A left pericardiotomy can be performed using bipolar electrocautery
typically two to three centimeters away from the phrenic nerve at the level of the hilum, and then proceeds directly inferiorly or posteriorly towards the diaphragm along the lung edge.

We then test the pulmonary veins for conduction, starting with the left superior pulmonary vein, followed by the bifurcation, and finally the inferior pulmonary vein.
